message:
feat(lexical): migrate add_conversation* onto packet-driven pipeline [coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.32]

Adds TantivyIndex::add_messages_from_packet, the packet-driven
counterpart to add_messages_with_conversation_id_and_batch_hook, plus
the helpers cass_doc_context_from_packet and
cass_document_for_packet_message that derive lexical doc context from
ConversationPacket projections instead of re-walking the raw
NormalizedConversation.

The in-tree convenience entrypoints add_conversation and
add_conversation_with_id now route through the packet pipeline. The
legacy NormalizedMessage-driven add_messages* methods stay in place
for indexer/mod.rs callers (still on the legacy path until that
file's exclusive lock is released).

Equivalence gate (per ibuuh.32 acceptance):
- packet_driven_lexical_pipeline_matches_legacy_for_normalized_conv
  reconstructs the CassDocument list both pipelines feed into Tantivy
  and asserts byte-for-byte equality on every stored field
  (agent/workspace/source_path/conversation_id/source_id/origin_kind/
  origin_host/title/content/msg_idx/created_at). Doc counts also
  match. Pins remote-host provenance round-trip so a regression in
  source_id or origin_host normalization on either side fails the test.
- add_conversation_with_id_packet_path_emits_expected_doc_count
  pins the convenience entrypoint behavior end-to-end.

19/19 src/search/tantivy.rs lib tests + 53/53 search_wildcard_fallback
integration tests green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
